preamble: The transformation from a palatalized voiceless alveolar fricative ([sʲ]) to a voiceless alveolo-palatal fricative ([ɕ]) represents a historical sound change where the secondary palatalization of an alveolar consonant becomes its primary articulation, shifting the place of articulation further back towards the palate.
phoneticEffects: Palatalization, Fronting (of the tongue body), Change in place of articulation from alveolar to alveolo-palatal

examples.note: The Proto-Slavic *s before front vowels, as in *sěno 'hay', palatalized to /sʲ/ in Old Polish, which subsequently 'softened' or advanced to /ɕ/ in Modern Polish.

sources: Padgett, J. & Żygis, M. (2007). The evolution of sibilants in Polish and Russian. Journal of Slavic Linguistics, 15(2), 291–324.
